senators.name: Jon Ossoff
senators.nerdReason: B.S.F.S. from Georgetown University School of Foreign Service and M.Sc. from London School of Economics. Career as investigative journalist and documentary filmmaker, producing investigations into corruption, war crimes, and fraud. Deep analytical and research skills honed through investigative work. LSE master's degree reflects rigorous academic training in political economy.
education: B.S.F.S., Georgetown University School of Foreign Service
experience: National Security Aide to Rep. Hank Johnson

senators.name: Raphael Warnock
senators.nerdReason: B.A. in psychology from Morehouse College, M.Div., M.Phil., and Ph.D. from Union Theological Seminary. A doctorate demonstrates deep commitment to research methodology and scholarly rigor. Senior pastor of Ebenezer Baptist Church (MLK's former church). Combines pastoral leadership with intellectual depth and evidence-based moral argumentation.

GA-01.name: Buddy Carter
GA-01.nerdReason: B.S. in pharmacy from University of Georgia. Only pharmacist serving in Congress. Owned and operated pharmacies for over 32 years. Pharmacy education requires strong science, chemistry, and analytical skills. Applies pharmaceutical expertise directly to healthcare and drug pricing policy.

GA-02.name: Sanford Bishop
GA-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Morehouse College and J.D. from Emory University School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Long-tenured in Congress with deep appropriations expertise. Solid policy knowledge but more consensus-builder than analytical policy wonk.
education: J.D., Emory University School of Law

GA-03.name: Brian Jack
GA-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Pepperdine University. Career as White House political advisor under Trump administration. Political operations background with no analytical, technical, or research-driven policy approach.

GA-04.name: Hank Johnson
GA-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Clark College and J.D. from Texas Southern University. Career as attorney, magistrate judge, and county commissioner. Legal background with focus on judiciary issues in Congress. Solid but not distinctly analytical or evidence-driven.

GA-05.name: Nikema Williams
GA-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Jacksonville University. Career in political organizing and state politics. Chair of the Democratic Party of Georgia. Strong political organizer but no analytical or research-driven policy background.

GA-06.name: Lucy McBath
GA-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from Virginia State University. Career as Delta Air Lines flight attendant and gun safety advocate after the murder of her son, Jordan Davis. Became national spokesperson for Everytown for Gun Safety. Passionate advocacy driven by personal experience rather than analytical frameworks.
experience: National Spokesperson, Everytown for Gun Safety

GA-07.name: Rich McCormick
GA-07.nerdReason: B.S. from Oregon State University, M.D. from Morehouse School of Medicine, and M.B.A. from National University. Emergency room physician at Northside Hospital. Marine helicopter pilot and Navy officer with 20+ years of military service including combat deployments. Triple-credentialed with medical, business, and military analytical expertise. One of the most technically credentialed members of the House.
experience: U.S. Marine Corps, Helicopter Pilot (20+ years)
Combat deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan

GA-08.name: Austin Scott
GA-08.notNerdReason: B.B.A. from University of Georgia. Career in insurance and risk management before entering politics. Business background provides practical skills but governance approach is not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.

GA-09.name: Andrew Clyde
GA-09.notNerdReason: B.B.A. and M.B.A. from University of Georgia. U.S. Navy veteran. Career as firearms retailer (Clyde Armory). Business education and military service provide structured thinking but governance approach is ideologically driven rather than analytically rigorous.

GA-10.name: Mike Collins
GA-10.notNerdReason: Attended Georgia Military College. Career in trucking and business. No college degree. No analytical, technical, or evidence-based policy background.

GA-11.name: Barry Loudermilk
GA-11.notNerdReason: B.S. in information technology from Wayland Baptist University. Air Force veteran (communications). IT degree and military communications background provide some technical foundation. Founded an IT consulting company. However, governance approach not notably data-driven or evidence-based.
education: B.S. Information Technology, Wayland Baptist University

GA-12.name: Rick Allen
GA-12.notNerdReason: B.S. in building construction from Auburn University. Founded and operated R.W. Allen & Associates construction company for 30+ years. Technical degree in construction provides engineering-adjacent analytical skills. Practical builder background but legislative approach not distinctly research-driven.

GA-13.name: David Scott
GA-13.nerdReason: B.A. from Florida A&M University and M.B.A. from the University of Pennsylvania's Wharton School of Business. Wharton MBA is one of the most analytically rigorous business programs in the world. Career in advertising and business. As Chairman of the House Agriculture Committee, applies analytical business expertise to complex agricultural and financial policy.
